changes.value:
## What

`update_window_title` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:49` hardcodes `"{APP_TITLE} — {name}"` where `name` comes from `handle.ctx.name()` — which is just `KanbanContext::derive_context_name`, the filesystem path stem above `.kanban` (e.g. `/home/user/my-project/.kanban` → `"my-project"`).

The board entity already has a `name` field (`board.yaml` declares `search_display_field: name`). The entity system has `EntityDef.search_display_field` to resolve display names. But `update_window_title` bypasses all of it.

### Current (wrong)
```rust
fn update_window_title(app: &AppHandle, label: &str, board_name: Option<&str>) {
    let title = match board_name {
        Some(name) if !name.is_empty() => format!("{APP_TITLE} \u{2014} {name}"),
        _ => APP_TITLE.to_string(),
    };
    ...
}
```

Callers pass `handle.ctx.name()` (path stem) as `board_name`.

### Fix

Change `update_window_title` to take a board entity (or read it from the entity context) and resolve the display name using `search_display_field` from the board's `EntityDef`. Fall back to `ctx.name()` (path stem) only if the entity or field is unavailable.

- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — refactor `update_window_title` to accept a `&KanbanContext` (or `&EntityContext` + `&FieldsContext`), read the board entity, resolve its `search_display_field`, and use that as the window title
- Callers in `create_window_impl` (~line 756), `dispatch_command_internal` BoardSwitch (~line 1070) and BoardClose (~line 1095), and `restore_windows` (~line 789) — update to pass the context instead of `handle.ctx.name()`
- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` `rebuild_menu` (~line 437) — the `WindowInfo.title` comes from `w.title()` which reads the OS window title, so it automatically picks up whatever `update_window_title` sets. No change needed here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Window title shows the board entity's `name` field value, not the filesystem directory name
- [ ] If the board entity has no `name` field or it's empty, falls back to the path stem (current behavior)
- [ ] Renaming a board (updating its `name` field) updates the window title on next board switch or entity flush
- [ ] Window menu entries reflect the entity-driven title (they read from OS window title)

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions
- [ ] Manual: create a board in `~/my-project/`, set board name to "My Cool Project" → window title shows "SwissArmyHammer — My Cool Project", not "SwissArmyHammer — my-project"
Refactor: update_window_title should use board entity display name, not path stem

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,36 +1,27 @@
 ## What

-`update_window_title` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:49` hardcodes `"{APP_TITLE} — {name}"` where `name` comes from `handle.ctx.name()` — which is just `KanbanContext::derive_context_name`, the filesystem path stem above `.kanban` (e.g. `/home/user/my-project/.kanban` → `"my-project"`).
-
-The board entity already has a `name` field (`board.yaml` declares `search_display_field: name`). The entity system has `EntityDef.search_display_field` to resolve display names. But `update_window_title` bypasses all of it.
+`update_window_title` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:49` hardcodes a string format using `handle.ctx.name()` — a filesystem path stem. Meanwhile, `{{entity.display_name}}` already exists in `scope_commands.rs` via `TemplateParams` and `resolve_name_template`. The entire template system resolves entity display names for command names, menu items, etc. The window title should use the same mechanism.

 ### Current (wrong)
 ```rust
 fn update_window_title(app: &AppHandle, label: &str, board_name: Option<&str>) {
-    let title = match board_name {
-        Some(name) if !name.is_empty() => format!("{APP_TITLE} \u{2014} {name}"),
-        _ => APP_TITLE.to_string(),
-    };
-    ...
+    // board_name = handle.ctx.name() = path stem, not entity display name
 }
 ```

-Callers pass `handle.ctx.name()` (path stem) as `board_name`.
-
 ### Fix

-Change `update_window_title` to take a board entity (or read it from the entity context) and resolve the display name using `search_display_field` from the board's `EntityDef`. Fall back to `ctx.name()` (path stem) only if the entity or field is unavailable.
+Use the board entity and `resolve_name_template` (or just read the board entity's `search_display_field` directly via the entity context) to get the display name. The `TemplateParams` struct already has `entity_name` (the entity's `name` field value) and `context_name` (the directory stem). Window title should use `entity_name`, falling back to `context_name`.

-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — refactor `update_window_title` to accept a `&KanbanContext` (or `&EntityContext` + `&FieldsContext`), read the board entity, resolve its `search_display_field`, and use that as the window title
-- Callers in `create_window_impl` (~line 756), `dispatch_command_internal` BoardSwitch (~line 1070) and BoardClose (~line 1095), and `restore_windows` (~line 789) — update to pass the context instead of `handle.ctx.name()`
-- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` `rebuild_menu` (~line 437) — the `WindowInfo.title` comes from `w.title()` which reads the OS window title, so it automatically picks up whatever `update_window_title` sets. No change needed here.
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — refactor `update_window_title` to read the board entity's display name from the entity context (the board entity's `name` field, per `search_display_field: name` in `board.yaml`). Fall back to `ctx.name()` only when the entity isn't available.
+- Callers at `create_window_impl` (~line 756), `dispatch_command_internal` BoardSwitch (~line 1070), BoardClose (~line 1095), and `restore_windows` (~line 789) — pass the entity context or resolved display name instead of `handle.ctx.name()`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Window title shows the board entity's `name` field value, not the filesystem directory name
-- [ ] If the board entity has no `name` field or it's empty, falls back to the path stem (current behavior)
-- [ ] Renaming a board (updating its `name` field) updates the window title on next board switch or entity flush
-- [ ] Window menu entries reflect the entity-driven title (they read from OS window title)
+- [ ] Window title uses the board entity's display name (the `name` field value), not the filesystem path stem
+- [ ] Falls back to path stem if entity unavailable
+- [ ] Window menu entries reflect the entity-driven title
+- [ ] Consistent with how `{{entity.display_name}}` resolves in command names

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions
-- [ ] Manual: create a board in `~/my-project/`, set board name to "My Cool Project" → window title shows "SwissArmyHammer — My Cool Project", not "SwissArmyHammer — my-project"
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: board with `name: "My Project"` in a dir called `foo/` → title shows "SwissArmyHammer — My Project", not "SwissArmyHammer — foo"
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,27 +1,23 @@
 ## What

-`update_window_title` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:49` hardcodes a string format using `handle.ctx.name()` — a filesystem path stem. Meanwhile, `{{entity.display_name}}` already exists in `scope_commands.rs` via `TemplateParams` and `resolve_name_template`. The entire template system resolves entity display names for command names, menu items, etc. The window title should use the same mechanism.
+`update_window_title` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:49` hardcodes a string format using `handle.ctx.name()` — a filesystem path stem. Meanwhile, `{{entity.display_name}}` already exists in `scope_commands.rs` via `TemplateParams` and `resolve_name_template`. The window title should use the board entity's display name — the same data everything else uses.

-### Current (wrong)
-```rust
-fn update_window_title(app: &AppHandle, label: &str, board_name: Option<&str>) {
-    // board_name = handle.ctx.name() = path stem, not entity display name
-}
-```
+There is no fallback needed. The board entity's `name` field is set to the directory name during `init board`. If there's a board, it has a display name. If there's no board, the title is just the app name.

 ### Fix

-Use the board entity and `resolve_name_template` (or just read the board entity's `search_display_field` directly via the entity context) to get the display name. The `TemplateParams` struct already has `entity_name` (the entity's `name` field value) and `context_name` (the directory stem). Window title should use `entity_name`, falling back to `context_name`.
+Replace `update_window_title`'s `board_name: Option<&str>` (currently fed by `handle.ctx.name()`, a path stem) with reading the board entity's display name from the entity context. The board entity's `search_display_field: name` in `board.yaml` declares which field is the display name.

-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — refactor `update_window_title` to read the board entity's display name from the entity context (the board entity's `name` field, per `search_display_field: name` in `board.yaml`). Fall back to `ctx.name()` only when the entity isn't available.
-- Callers at `create_window_impl` (~line 756), `dispatch_command_internal` BoardSwitch (~line 1070), BoardClose (~line 1095), and `restore_windows` (~line 789) — pass the entity context or resolved display name instead of `handle.ctx.name()`.
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — refactor `update_window_title` to read the board entity's display name from the entity context
+- Callers at `create_window_impl` (~line 756), `dispatch_command_internal` BoardSwitch (~line 1070), BoardClose (~line 1095), and `restore_windows` (~line 789) — pass the entity context instead of `handle.ctx.name()`

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Window title uses the board entity's display name (the `name` field value), not the filesystem path stem
-- [ ] Falls back to path stem if entity unavailable
+- [ ] Window title uses the board entity's display name (the `name` field value)
+- [ ] No separate path-stem fallback — the entity IS the fallback (init sets it to the dir name)
 - [ ] Window menu entries reflect the entity-driven title
 - [ ] Consistent with how `{{entity.display_name}}` resolves in command names

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions
-- [ ] Manual: board with `name: "My Project"` in a dir called `foo/` → title shows "SwissArmyHammer — My Project", not "SwissArmyHammer — foo"
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: board with `name: "My Project"` in a dir called `foo/` → title shows "SwissArmyHammer — My Project"
+- [ ] Manual: rename board name field → window title updates on next refresh
\ No newline at end of file
